description: Single-user MCP server that ingests NYC family-friendly events into SQLite + FTS5 and exposes them as MCP tools over streamable HTTP, gated by an OAuth 2.1 + PKCE shim. See CLAUDE.md for architecture and README.md for setup.
canonical_status_source: CLAUDE.md '## Phase roadmap' is the single source of truth for phase status. This file is a derived session-tracker snapshot — when a phase ships, update CLAUDE.md first, then reconcile this file and progress.md to match.

features.name: Phase 1 — NYC permit ingest core
features.description: Event model + stable compute_id, split SQLite stores (events + oauth) with idempotent migrations, FTS5 search, and the nyc_permitted_events (tvpp-9vvx) source with aggressive permit-registry filtering. ~700 events / 60-day window, all low_confidence.
features.evidence: tests/test_db.py, tests/test_nyc_permitted_events_parse.py green; source live in ENABLED_SOURCES.

features.name: MCP server + OAuth shim + HTTP security baseline
features.description: FastMCP over streamable HTTP at root, OAuth 2.1 + PKCE consent shim using MCP_AUTH_TOKEN, claude.ai web connector compatibility (browser-probe 200, DNS-rebinding disabled, forwarded-proto handling), and the Checkpoint C security bundle (secrets.compare_digest, redirect allowlist, consent CSP headers, per-IP rate limiter, token TTL).
features.evidence: tests/test_security_fixes.py green; see CLAUDE.md 'HTTP security baseline' and 'claude.ai web connector quirks'.

features.name: Missing-event detection (possible cancellations)
features.description: Future events that disappear from a source's feed are flagged (possibly_cancelled) rather than deleted, with four guard layers: hard-failure opt-out, circuit breaker for silent partial fetches, self-healing flag/clear, and a read-time grace window. Opt-in via Source.window_days; mommy_poppins stays excluded.
features.evidence: tests/test_missing_detection.py, tests/test_weekend_window.py green; see CLAUDE.md 'Missing-event detection'.

features.name: Phase 2 — editorial scrapers
features.description: Real descriptions, URLs, and age ranges from venue sources via the source-adder recipe (fixture -> parser -> registry -> parser test -> docs). Live: Mommy Poppins, BPL, Brooklyn Children's Museum, Green-Wood Cemetery, Prospect Park Alliance, NY Transit Museum, Brooklyn Army Terminal. Buildable backlog cleared.
features.evidence: Eight sources in ENABLED_SOURCES with matching test_<source>_parse.py fixtures; SOURCES-BACKLOG.md statuses synced. Rejected: Time Out NY Kids, Coney Island USA.

features.name: Docker packaging + deploy (Checkpoint D)
features.description: Multi-arch (amd64 + arm64) GHCR image built by docker-publish.yml, non-root uid 10001, hardcoded /data DB paths, 127.0.0.1-bound port behind Tailscale Funnel, Watchtower-scoped auto-update, TCP healthcheck, host-cron nightly ingest via docker exec.
features.evidence: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.dev.yml, .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml; see CLAUDE.md 'Docker conventions'.

features.name: Session-handoff harness tooling
features.description: Repo-tailored init.sh, feature-list.json, progress.md, and session-handoff.md so web/remote sessions can bootstrap, verify, and hand off cleanly. Replaces the default Claude templates with project-specific content.
features.evidence: init.sh runs pytest + ruff via .venv; feature-list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md tailored from the root docs. Merged to main via PR #13.

features.name: Phase 3 — enrichment pipeline scaffold + caching layer
features.description: Second nightly pass that runs after ingest and enriches rows without complicating source fetch(). Adds a geocode cache (stable, no TTL, keyed by lookup string) and a weather cache (short TTL, keyed by NWS gridpoint), leaning toward new namespaced tables in events.db. Foundational for geocoding and weather. See PHASE-3-PLAN.md 'Architecture: second nightly pass + caching layer'.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md. Sequencing step 1, alongside tech debt.

features.name: Phase 3 — tech-debt bundle (#4/#5/#6)
features.description: Server-touching cleanup done in one pass: #4 FTS5 VACUUM footgun (operational/doc), #5 split the consent password from the master bearer (decouple the one-env-var/two-roles MCP_AUTH_TOKEN), #6 efficiency/hygiene grab-bag. Bundled because the read-path enrichment work is already in server.py. See PHASE-3-PLAN.md Workstream C.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md Workstream C. Issues #4/#5/#6.

features.name: Phase 3 — geocoding, neighborhood, distance-from-home
features.description: Backfill lat/lng for rows lacking coords (US Census geocoder primary, Nominatim fallback; geocode by venue_name + borough + 'NY', cached). Map to Event.neighborhood via NYC NTA / borough+ZIP. Add a near_me / sort-by-distance search affordance once a home location is configured (env or config row). See PHASE-3-PLAN.md A1.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md A1. Sequencing step 2.

features.name: Phase 3 — indoor/outdoor flag (heuristic)
features.description: New nullable enum column (indoor/outdoor/mixed/unknown) via idempotent ALTER TABLE migration. Per-source default (museums/libraries indoor; zoos/gardens/parks outdoor; mixed venues unknown) plus a keyword override on title/description. Explicitly heuristic, NOT AI-derived. See PHASE-3-PLAN.md A2.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md A2. Sequencing step 3.

features.name: Phase 3 — weather on outdoor events
features.description: Attach a compact forecast (temp range + precip/condition) to upcoming outdoor events within the ~7-day window via NWS api.weather.gov (free, no key). Needs coords (feat-009) and the indoor/outdoor flag (feat-010); cache forecasts by NWS gridpoint with a short TTL. See PHASE-3-PLAN.md A3.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md A3. Sequencing step 4.

features.name: Phase 3 — venue expansion (Workstream B)
features.description: Add venue sources via the source-adder recipe with window_days opt-in and an indoor/outdoor default per source: WCS zoos + aquarium, museums (Intrepid, Brooklyn/Queens Museum, SI Children's, NYSCI), Brooklyn Botanic Garden, NYPL + Queens Public Library, parks programming (City Parks Foundation/SummerStage Kids, Hudson River Park). Adopt a Playwright headless _render fallback (separate ingest image) only for probed sources with no structured feed. Brooklyn Cyclones parked here. See PHASE-3-PLAN.md Workstream B.
features.evidence: Plan only — PHASE-3-PLAN.md Workstream B. Sequencing step 5.
